data.title: Cluster Reply partners with Riverty to develop AI-powered customer service platform in 100 days as part of Riverty's AI-first strategy.
data.description: In TURIN, Italy, Cluster Reply, a Reply company focusing on digital platform solutions using Microsoft technologies, has teamed up with fintech company Riverty to fast-track the launch of an innovative customer service platform in just 100 days. This collaboration aligns with Riverty's goal to excel in AI-driven financial services. The new platform enables Riverty to offer effective and empathetic customer assistance across all channels.

data.title: KingSmith marks 10th anniversary with "One Endless Journey" campaign, promoting innovation in fitness tech.
data.description: A company specializing in compact fitness technology, KingSmith, based in Dallas, marked its 10th year in the industry with the introduction of "One Endless Journey" campaign. The initiative aims to recognize a decade of groundbreaking innovation and motivate individuals worldwide to kick start their fitness journey.

data.title: Hisense hosts 2025 Laser Display Conference in Qingdao with theme "Boundless," marking a decade of innovation.
data.description: In Qingdao, China, on September 19, 2025, Hisense, a prominent global brand in consumer electronics and home appliances, organized the 2025 Laser Display Technology and Industry Development Conference with the theme "Boundless." The event marked a decade of innovation for the company, reinforcing its position in the industry.

data.title: PEPE price falls by 6% to $0.0000107 as whales buy in despite market selloff; RSI at 53.39 with bullish MACD.
data.description: PEPE's current trading price is at $0.0000107 following a recent decrease. Technical analysis suggests conflicting signals, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) standing at 53.39. However, there is a positive sign as the Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) shows bullish momentum forming.

data.title: Video game leaders from 4J Studios and Nintendo commend Scotland at DICE Europe conference. Over 200 senior executives in attendance.
data.description: In Scotland, the video games industry leaders celebrated the country's contribution to the global sector during the conclusion of DICE, the annual European video-game conference organized by the Academy of Interactive Arts and Sciences. Backed by the Scottish Government and Scottish Enterprise, DICE Europe commenced at the Intercontinental in Edinburgh with a keynote address by Nintendo's Mr. Satoru Shibata. The event saw the gathering of approximately 200 high-level executives, showcasing Scotland's importance in the industry.

data.title: 2025 ISG Women in Digital Award winners in EMEA announced, including leaders from Shell, Pfizer, BBC, Genpact, Santander, and WNS.
data.description: In London, prominent figures from Shell, Pfizer, BBC, Genpact, Santander, and WNS have emerged victorious at the 2025 ISG Women in Digital Awards for EMEA. These leaders have been recognized for their outstanding contributions to the digital industry. This prestigious award highlights the achievements of women in the field, showcasing the talent and expertise of individuals from diverse organizations. The winners from these renowned companies demonstrate the importance of promoting diversity and excellence in the digital sector.

data.title: Adastra named Elite Partner in Databricks Program, after 6 years of collaboration. Focus on AI, data & analytics solutions.
data.description: A company based in Toronto, Adastra, was recently honored as an Elite Partner within the Databricks Partner Program, signifying a significant milestone in their ongoing partnership. This recognition underscores Adastra's proficiency in providing AI, data, and analytics solutions using the Databricks platform, emphasizing their dedication to assisting companies in translating intricate data into tangible business outcomes. Having collaborated for over six years, Adastra has solidified its position as a reputable partner of Databricks.

data.title: Laos plans to use surplus hydropower for crypto mining to tackle debt. Rising debt due to energy boom from Chinese loans.
data.description: Laos is venturing into cryptocurrency mining to alleviate its increasing debt burden and utilize excess energy, a report revealed. The country aims to capitalize on its surplus power generated from numerous hydropower projects along the Mekong River, earning it the title of the "battery of Southeast Asia." Despite the energy abundance, Laos faces a substantial debt increase primarily from Chinese loans and foreign investments in dam construction. In response, the government has started granting licenses for domestic cryptocurrency trading platforms and mining activities. The objective is to transform the surplus energy into economic gains and mitigate financial strain. More details are accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Credit card usage in Hong Kong experienced a substantial surge in Q2 2025, with transactions and circulation seeing significant growth.
data.description: In the second quarter of 2025, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority has observed a remarkable surge in the use of credit cards. Both the number of transactions and the overall circulation of credit cards have demonstrated substantial growth when compared to preceding periods.
